data.title: [pre-bash dispatcher]: every block path emits spurious 'No such file' + 'return from function' lines
data.description:
## Incident Overview

Every PreToolUse hook block path (record-test-status-guard, bypass-sentinel, tickets-tracker-guard, etc.) ends with two spurious error lines after the legitimate BLOCKED diagnostic:

  <PLUGIN_CACHE>/hooks/dispatchers/pre-bash.sh: line 139: : No such file or directory
  <PLUGIN_CACHE>/hooks/dispatchers/pre-bash.sh: line 139: return: can only return from a function or sourced script

The block itself still works correctly (exit 2 is preserved; the tool call is actually blocked), so the errors are cosmetic — but they make every legitimate block look like the dispatcher is broken, create debugging noise, and would mask real future ERR-trap errors.

Expected: block output ends cleanly after the BLOCKED diagnostic, no trailing errors.

Actual: two trailing error lines on every block regardless of which guard function fired.

Reproduction: any Bash tool call that triggers a dispatcher block. Captured examples this session across three separate guard paths during bug investigation.

Hypothesis: the shared hook-error-handler ERR trap fires on the intentional exit 2 and executes either an unset variable as a command (producing the colon-prefix message) and a return keyword at top-level script scope. pre-bash.sh line 139 area is inside the main dispatch block, not inside a function; the EXIT trap is cleared before exit but an ERR trap may still fire between _dispatch_exit assignment and exit.

Affected: plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/pre-bash.sh (line 139 area) and plugins/dso/hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh. Related ticket 530e-13d8 already fixed the EXIT trap converting exit 2 to 0; this is a separate surface.

Impact: priority 3 (cosmetic/DX) — every block interaction displays confusing errors that make the dispatcher look broken. User noticed and requested it be filed.
